stories.title: Cubs 6, Giants 1 — Crow-Armstrong Homers on First Pitch, Three-Game Streak
stories.angle: Pete Crow-Armstrong launched the first pitch he saw Saturday into the stands for a leadoff homer — his 12th of the season — as the Cubs beat the Giants 6-1. Ben Brown was excellent with 5.2 IP and 1 hit allowed. The Cubs have won three straight and claimed their first series win in 11 tries. Craig Counsell says stop asking if the Cubs are "back."

article.headline: Cubs 6, Giants 1: Crow-Armstrong Homers on First Pitch, Three-Game Streak

stories.title: Skubal Post-Mortem — Rust Shown, Health Confirmed, Deadline Watch Continues
stories.angle: Tarik Skubal allowed 3 runs in 4⅔ innings in his first start back from 38-day IL stint Saturday, losing to the Guardians 3-1. He was rusty — took a Daniel Schneemann 2-run homer — but threw 80 pitches and the health concern is resolved. Skubal: "I don't want to play into that narrative." The two-time Cy Young's trade value is still in play for August 3.

article.headline: Skubal Returns: Rust Shown, Health Confirmed, Deadline Watch Continues

stories.title: Schwarber-Alvarez Tied at 24 HR — Derby Weekend on Deck
stories.angle: Kyle Schwarber and Yordan Alvarez are deadlocked at 24 home runs apiece, tied for the MLB lead. Byron Buxton sits third at 21. The Home Run Derby is July 13 at Citizens Bank Park — Schwarber's home park. The race to 60+ is now officially a two-player story heading into the All-Star break.

article.headline: Schwarber and Alvarez Tied at 24 HR — Derby at Schwarber's Home Park Coming Fast

stories.title: Braves 45-23 but Acuña to IL — Hamstring Strain Clouds Best Record in Baseball
stories.angle: Ronald Acuña Jr. is on the injured list with a left hamstring strain, dealing a blow to the team with the best record in baseball. The Braves had gone 45-23 before dropping three straight games without their star. Chris Sale leads the majors with 92 strikeouts and 8 wins; Matt Olson has 20 HR and 51 RBI. Can Atlanta hold without Acuña until the deadline?

article.headline: Braves' Best Record in Baseball Under Pressure as Acuña Hits the IL

stories.title: All-Star Voting Open — Ben Rice in Line for First Selection, Judge Likely a Scratch
stories.angle: Phase 1 of MLB All-Star voting runs through June 25, with the game July 14 in Philadelphia. Ben Rice (.300/.393/.638, 17 HR, 181 wRC+ in 57 games) is on track to be the Yankees' starter at first base — his first All-Star honor. Aaron Judge may earn enough votes to win a starting spot, but would be an injury scratch with his rib stress fracture. The Midsummer Classic takes shape.

article.headline: All-Star Voting Is Open: Ben Rice's Moment, Judge's Situation, and Derby Intrigue
